day4_morning_start.name: Me
day4_morning_start.text: *Day 4 morning. I woke up before the alarm. 5:38 AM.*

day4_morning_start_2.name: Me
day4_morning_start_2.text: *The writing on the wall is still there. 'Get out of here.' It wasn't a dream.*

day4_morning_start_3.name: Me
day4_morning_start_3.text: *I traced the letters with my fingertips. Scratch marks from fingernails. Deep. Too deep to have been done in one pass.*

day4_morning_start_4.name: Me
day4_morning_start_4.text: *...The skin under my nails is torn. It wasn't like that before I fell asleep last night.*

day4_morning_start_5.name: Me
day4_morning_start_5.text: *Could it be -- did I write this in my sleep?*

day4_morning_start_6.name: Me
day4_morning_start_6.text: *Or -- was I awake and just can't remember?*

day4_morning_start_7.name: Me
day4_morning_start_7.text: *I stood in front of the mirror. ...It's fogged up again. I reached to wipe it, then stopped.*

day4_morning_start_8.name: Me
day4_morning_start_8.text: *Through the fogged mirror, a shape is reflected. Blurry. The outline of a person. ...It should be my outline. So why does it feel -- unfamiliar?*

day4_morning_start_9.name: Me
day4_morning_start_9.text: *Something seems to overlap through the fog. Another face layered over mine. ...Must be my imagination.*

day4_morning_start_10.name: Me
day4_morning_start_10.text: *...I didn't wipe it. For some reason, I'm afraid to see my face. Until yesterday, I thought I just couldn't be bothered. Today -- I'm not so sure.*

day4_morning_start_12.name: Me
day4_morning_start_12.text: *I washed up halfheartedly. Brushed my teeth with my back to the mirror. I know it's not normal. Not being able to look at a mirror.*

day4_morning_start_13.name: Me
day4_morning_start_13.text: *My hands tremble as I put on my uniform. I fumbled the buttons three times.*

day4_morning_start_14.name: Me
day4_morning_start_14.text: *Morning sunlight streams through the curtains. A bright day. But -- someone is standing on the sidewalk below the window. A suit. Sunglasses. Arms crossed, looking up this way.*

day4_morning_start_15.name: Me
day4_morning_start_15.text: *Two seconds. I blinked. Gone. ...Was anyone really there?*

day4_morning_start_16.name: Me
day4_morning_start_16.text: *Yuna's words come back to me. 'There are always the same people around this school. Not parents, not residents.'*

day4_morning_start_17.name: Me
day4_morning_start_17.text: *I stopped at the front door. Through the gap under the door, I can see a shadow. Two feet's worth. Someone is standing right outside.*

day4_morning_start_18.name: Me
day4_morning_start_18.text: *Five seconds. Ten seconds. I held my breath. The shadow doesn't move.*

day4_morning_start_19.name: Me
day4_morning_start_19.text: *Fifteen seconds. The shadow disappears. Footsteps -- none. It vanished without a sound.*

day4_morning_start_20.name: Me
day4_morning_start_20.text: *...I opened the door. Nobody in the hallway. A bright morning hallway. Nobody.*

day4_morning_start_21.name: Me
day4_morning_start_21.text: *A white envelope lies on the floor in front of the door. No name on it.*

day4_morning_start_22.name: Me
day4_morning_start_22.text: *I opened it. A single sheet of paper inside. Printed text: **'Today's schedule: Proceed as normal. -- Management Division'***

day4_morning_start_23.name: Me
day4_morning_start_23.text: *...Management Division? This apartment building doesn't have a management division.*

day4_morning_start_24.name: Me
day4_morning_start_24.text: *I put the envelope in my bag. My hands are shaking.*

day4_morning_commute.name: Me
day4_morning_commute.text: *On the way to school. A notification popped up from the safety app on my phone.*

day4_morning_commute_3.name: Me
day4_morning_commute_3.text: *Blink. It vanished. Looking again, it's a normal notification. 'Good morning, {name}!'*

day4_morning_commute_4.name: Me
day4_morning_commute_4.text: *...What did it just say? 'Subject'? ...Must be a bug in the security app. No. It's not a bug. Yuna showed me yesterday. What this app really is.*

day4_morning_commute_5.name: Me
day4_morning_commute_5.text: *I stare at my phone again. The app icon. 'Safety App.' An app I never installed. An app that can't be deleted.*

day4_morning_commute_7.name: Me
day4_morning_commute_7.text: *...Of course. Because this isn't my app. It's an app that manages me.*

day4_morning_commute_8.name: Me
day4_morning_commute_8.text: *Midway through my commute. I stopped at a crosswalk. Students walking toward me from the other side. Five of them. Side by side.*

day4_morning_commute_9.name: Me
day4_morning_commute_9.text: *They're all walking at the same speed. Same stride length. Same angle of arm swing. As if -- running on the same program.*

day4_morning_gate.name: Me
day4_morning_gate.text: *School front gate. Students pass through in small groups.*

day4_morning_gate_2.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_2.text: *The students' expressions are... all the same.*

day4_morning_gate_3.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_3.text: ***They're all smiling. The same smile. Corners of their mouths raised at the same angle.***

day4_morning_gate_4.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_4.text: *I watched one of them. The moment they stepped through the gate -- their expression changed. From blank to smiling. Like a switch flipped on.*

day4_morning_gate_5.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_5.text: *Another one. Same thing. The moment they passed the gate pillar -- smile. Yet another. Having a serious conversation in front of the gate -- the instant they crossed through, both simultaneously wore the same smile.*

day4_morning_gate_6.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_6.text: ***Every single one. No exceptions. The moment they cross the boundary of the front gate -- they all wear the same face.***

day4_morning_gate_7.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_7.text: *Three girls pass through the gate. They turn their heads toward me simultaneously. Same angle. Same timing. Same smile.*

day4_morning_gate_9.name: Me
day4_morning_gate_9.text: *Eunsu is standing by the front gate. Leaning against it. Smiling. The same smile as the other students -- no. His is different. He's the only one genuinely smiling.*

day4_morning_eunsu_2.name: Eunsu
day4_morning_eunsu_2.text: "Let's walk to the classroom together today."

day4_morning_eunsu_3.name: Me
day4_morning_eunsu_3.text: *He links arms and stands beside me. Naturally. As if he's done this every day.*

day4_morning_eunsu_comply.name: Me
day4_morning_eunsu_comply.text: *I walked alongside Eunsu to the classroom. ...An escort -- or a convoy. I couldn't tell which.*

day4_morning_eunsu_refuse_2.name: Eunsu
day4_morning_eunsu_refuse_2.text: "It's better not to walk around alone in this school."

day4_morning_eunsu_refuse_4.name: Me
day4_morning_eunsu_refuse_4.text: *The weight of the word 'dangerous' has changed. Until yesterday, it was friendly advice. Today -- it's a warning.*

day4_morning_eunsu_refuse_5.name: Me
day4_morning_eunsu_refuse_5.text: *Eunsu follows behind me. Half a step back. The exact distance to never leave my field of vision.*

day4_morning_classroom_2.name: Me
day4_morning_classroom_2.text: ***Yuna's seat. Empty. No bag. No trace of her at all.***

day4_morning_classroom_3.name: Me
day4_morning_classroom_3.text: *The desk is clean. Too clean. Not a single piece of graffiti. As if no one has ever sat there.*

day4_morning_classroom_4.name: Me
day4_morning_classroom_4.text: *...Until yesterday, there were stickers on it. Photography club stickers. Those are gone too.*

day4_morning_sea.name: Han Sea
day4_morning_sea.text: "Yuna? ...She transferred. Back to her old school."

day4_morning_sea_4.name: Me
day4_morning_sea_4.text: *Yesterday, Yuna showed me three years' worth of evidence. And she suddenly transferred?*

day4_morning_sea_5.name: Me
day4_morning_sea_5.text: "You know Yuna Choi, right? The photography club junior."

day4_morning_sea_6.name: Boy student
day4_morning_sea_6.text: "...Yuna Choi? Was there someone by that name?"

day4_morning_sea_7.name: Girl student
day4_morning_sea_7.text: "Yuna...? Oh, maybe the one who transferred last year?"

day4_morning_sea_8.name: Me
day4_morning_sea_8.text: *Last year? Yuna was here until yesterday.*

day4_morning_sea_9.name: Me
day4_morning_sea_9.text: *The entire classroom is -- acting as if they've forgotten Yuna.*

day4_morning_sea_10.name: Me
day4_morning_sea_10.text: *...No, they haven't forgotten. It's as if she never existed.*

day4_morning_class_2.name: Me
day4_morning_class_2.text: *Eunsu stands at the lectern. Picks up the chalk. Writes 'Belonging' on the blackboard.*

day4_morning_class_3.name: Me
day4_morning_class_3.text: *...The attendance sheet lies open on the lectern. The space where Yuna's name should be -- is blank. I can see traces of correction tape.*

day4_morning_class_4.name: Eunsu
day4_morning_class_4.text: "Belonging means... safety. Being here means you're protected."

day4_morning_class_5.name: Eunsu
day4_morning_class_5.text: "To belong means -- someone is looking after you."

day4_morning_class_6.name: Eunsu
day4_morning_class_6.text: "But trying to leave this school... isn't easy."

day4_morning_class_7.name: Me
day4_morning_class_7.text: *His gaze locks onto me. He takes one step closer from the lectern.*

day4_morning_class_8.name: Eunsu
day4_morning_class_8.text: "Those who tried to leave... most of them regretted it."

day4_morning_class_10.name: Me
day4_morning_class_10.text: *Every pair of eyes in the classroom turns toward me. Thirty pairs. All smiling.*

day4_morning_class_11.name: Me
day4_morning_class_11.text: *...No, twenty-nine pairs. Only Yuna's seat is empty.*

day4_morning_class_12.name: Me
day4_morning_class_12.text: *The empty seat feels like -- an answer. What happens to those who try to leave.*
